title: beyeria viscosa var. genuina
intro: Beyeria viscosa var. genuina, commonly known as the sticky beeyeria or varnished beeyeria, is a plant species in the family Cephalotaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, known for its sticky resinous stems and leaves. The medicinal properties of Beyeria viscosa var. genuina include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as salves and creams to treat skin conditions.
